Key conclusions:
- **No over-claim:** manifest and `command-evidence.json` set `production_ready: false` (status `implemented_pending_review`); `production_ready_candidate: true` reflects only passing coverage/foundation validation, correctly gated behind this review + human approval.
- **Full consistency:** prompt baseline ↔ `registry.js:206` ↔ `dms-core-contract.json:3` ↔ `README.md:57` ↔ manifest snapshot ↔ live `closeout-pack-plan.json:413` all agree; distribution math is exact (phase 46+104, deliverable 8-types, micro-title 9, section rows) — all =150 and independently validated/tested at exit 0.
- **Boundaries upheld:** descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data enforced per-row and pack-level; Claude read-only and not final approver; Hermes used only for deterministic descriptor/receipt, not product runtime; CP178+ review-hardening blockers encoded.

The pack does NOT over-claim: manifest/command-evidence set production_ready=false at status implemented_pending_review, with production_ready_candidate=true reflecting only that coverage+foundation validations pass, gated behind this review and human final approval. All layers agree (prompt baseline, review-baseline, registry bindings, dms-core-contract.json, README, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, live closeout-pack-plan.json). Distribution math is exact across phase (46+104=150), deliverable (8 types=150), micro-title (9=150), and per-section row lists (150); each dimension is independently validated in validators.js and asserted in model.test.js, with npm test / rp06:dms-core:validate / validate recorded at exit 0. Descriptor-only, no-write, and no-real-data boundaries are uniformly enforced per row and at pack level (no API handler execution, no Hermes runtime receipts, no response/payload/cursor/unauthorized-data leakage). Authority boundary is upheld: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false), local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and runtime opening requires human approval in CP00-211+. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor/receipt handling, not product runtime. Review-hardening blockers (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, plus final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims) are encoded and mirrored in the contract; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON and tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob. Two non-blocking P3 observations recorded for downstream/human-approval awareness; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Pack is supported for closeout under the standing human-final-approval boundary.","findings":[{"id":"CP210-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"P03 API-surface sections are descriptor stubs; asserted no-leak guards are deferred design intent to enforce at runtime opening","file":"packages/dms/src/service.js","line":2807,"details":"The six RP06.P03 sections (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding) declare API-surface rows (request/response contracts, error-code taxonomy, pagination/filtering contract, serialization guard, unauthorized-data omission, API fixtures) as descriptor-only stubs. Guards such as api_response_payload_included=false, pagination_cursor_material_included=false, unauthorized_data_omitted=true, and serialization_guard_applied=true are asserted as static descriptor flags, not exercised against any executing handler. This is correctly disclosed (downstream_consumer_note.downstream_runtime_opened=false; authority_boundary.human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-211), so it is not a defect for this descriptor pack.","recommendation":"When CP00-211+ opens runtime for these P03 API surfaces, the human final approver should verify the real handlers actually honor every no-leak guard asserted here (no API response payloads/cursor material/error-taxonomy internals/unauthorized data leaked; serialization guard applied). No action required for CP00-210 closeout.","post_review_disposition":"Deferred to CP00-211+ runtime opening under human final approval; informational, no CP00-210 action."},{"id":"CP210-P3-02","severity":"P3","title":"RP06.P02.M08 micro-title is 'Hermes Evidence Packet' while the 4 rows in this pack are all test-type tail rows","file":"packages/dms/src/registry.js","line":1807,"details":"Because the pack boundary starts mid-micro-phase at RP06.P02.M08.S19, the M08 section contributes only its 4 tail rows (Unit test: happy/denied/review, Integration smoke case), all deliverable_type=test, even though the M08 micro_title is 'Hermes Evidence Packet' (its evidence head rows S01-S18 closed in CP00-209). Separately, the 4 hermes_evidence deliverable-type units live in P03.M02-M05 (S14 'Hermes API evidence'). The micro_title and deliverable_type dimensions are orthogonal and both independently validated to count 4, so this is internally consistent and not a defect; it is noted only because scanning by micro_title alone could be momentarily confusing.","recommendation":"No change required.
